leadText: Hammerlocke (Japanese: ナックルシティ Knuckle City) is a city situated in the center of the Galar region.

sections.title: Slogan
sections.text: Slogan [ edit | edit source ] A city of great history, living and thriving within castle walls that date back to the middle ages. (Japanese: 中世 の 城壁 を 活 かした 歴史 ある 街 A historic city that takes advantage of its castle walls from the middle ages. )

Caramel/Black, Red/Black, Blue/White $ 62,000 Salon [ edit | edit source ] Move Tutor [ edit | edit source ] Down the stairs leading to the moats on the leftmost part of the town, there is a Move Tutor who will teach Grass Pledge , Fire Pledge , or Water Pledge to any evolutionary form of first partner Pokémon . In addition, Grass Pledge can also be taught to Silvally . Move Type Cat. Grass Pledge Grass Special Fire Pledge Fire Special Water Pledge Water Special Friendship Rater [ edit | edit source ] Hammerlocke Vault [ edit | edit source ] Hammerlocke Vault is a building located on the west side of the city and is supervised by Raihan . It houses four tapestries depicting the history of Galar and the Darkest Day . The player 's Hammerlocke Gym mission takes place at the vault. Battle Café [ edit | edit source ] Main article: Battle Café The Battle Café is located on the west side of the city. Pokémon Center (South) [ edit | edit source ] This Pokémon Center is located in the south of the city, just north of the Wild Area entrance. Poké Mart [ edit | edit source ] This Poké Mart is located inside the Pokémon Center. Left cashier Poké Ball $ 200 Great Ball $ 600 Ultra Ball 5 Badges $ 800 Potion $ 200 Super Potion $ 700 Hyper Potion 4 Badges $ 1,500 Max Potion 7 Badges $ 2,500 Full Restore 8 Badges $ 3,000 Antidote $ 200 Burn Heal $ 200 Ice Heal $ 200 Awakening $ 200 Paralyze Heal $ 200 Full Heal 6 Badges $ 400 Revive $ 2,000 Repel $ 400 Super Repel 5 Badges $ 700 Max Repel 7 Badges $ 900 Poké Doll $ 300 Right cashier Dive Ball $ 1,000 Dusk Ball $ 1,000 Timer Ball $ 1,000 X Speed $ 1,000 X Accuracy $ 1,000 Guard Spec. $ 1,500 Dire Hit $ 1,000 BP Shop Rare Candy 20 BP PP Up 10 BP Power Bracer 10 BP Power Belt 10 BP Power Lens 10 BP Power Band 10 BP Power Anklet 10 BP Power Weight 10 BP Macho Brace 10 BP Destiny Knot 10 BP Razor Claw 10 BP Protector 10 BP Reaper Cloth 10 BP Whipped Dream 10 BP Sachet 10 BP HP Up 2 BP Protein 2 BP Iron 2 BP Calcium 2 BP Zinc 2 BP Carbos 2 BP Hammerlocke Stadium [ edit | edit source ] Main article: Hammerlocke Stadium Hammerlocke Stadium is the official Pokémon Gym of Hammerlocke. The Gym Leader , Raihan , specializes in Dragon-type Pokémon. He also specializes in Double Battles and weather manipulation. It is the last Gym that Gym Challengers have to face. He hands out the Dragon Badge to those who defeat him. The stadium also houses the Macro Cosmos Energy Plant , which can be accessed by taking an elevator from the lobby. The Energy Plant also has another elevator that leads to the tower summit at the top of the stadium. Hammerlocke Station [ edit | edit source ] Hammerlocke Station is located in the northeast of the city. The train can be ridden from here once the player has earned the Fairy Badge . The player can catch a train from this station to the following locations: Wedgehurst Station Wild Area Station ( Wild Area ) Motostoke Station Hulbury Station White Hill Station (once the player has earned the Dragon Badge and talked with Hop and Raihan, which only triggers after walking into the station and not from taking the train.) Wyndon Station (once the player has visited Wyndon) Armor Station (once the player has visited Armor Station from Wedgehurst Station) Crown Tundra Station (once the player has visited Crown Tundra Station from Wedgehurst Station) Poké Mart [ edit | edit source ] This Poké Mart is the eastmost storefront inside the station.
